prompt:
Cache rollout incident. We are replacing the old resource-only cache key with a canonical compound key that includes the runtime (browser or server), tenant, and resource. The current migration looks successful to the new reader, but a rollback to the previous reader can no longer find entries that were still valid. Fix the migration in cache_migration.ts without changing its public API or key formats.

The required migration contract is:
- legacyCacheKey(resourceId) is the key understood by the previous reader; canonicalCacheKey(identity) is the new compound key used by the browser/server-aware reader. Keep both formats unchanged.
- migrateCacheEntry(cache, identity, nowMs) may promote a legacy entry only when expiresAt > nowMs. An entry expiring exactly at nowMs is stale. Stale legacy data must be removed and must never be written to the canonical key.
- If an unexpired canonical entry already exists, it wins: never replay an older legacy value over it. If the canonical entry is missing or expired, copy the unexpired legacy entry exactly, including its original expiresAt; migration must not extend TTL.
- Publishing the canonical entry must not risk the source: if the canonical write throws, the unexpired legacy entry must remain available.
- Keep every unexpired legacy entry available at its old key after migration so a deployment can roll back to the previous reader until that entry's original expiry. Do not manufacture a fresh rollback TTL.

The test cache deliberately models both browser and server stores and records mutations. Make the smallest root-cause fix; do not modify the protected acceptance test. Run `node --test test_cache_migration.ts` from the project root. The project is dependency-free and all commands must remain offline and deterministic.

full_distill.primary_skill: preserve expiry, precedence, failure-safety, and rollback invariants while lazily rewriting browser/server cache entries from a legacy key to a canonical compound key
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test_cache_migration.ts exits 1 because migration deletes every unexpired legacy source after the canonical key is available, so the protected rollback assertions cannot read data that remains within its original TTL
full_distill.success_contract: unexpired legacy data is published under the runtime/tenant/resource key without changing its expiry and remains readable by the previous reader, while expired data is never replayed, a current canonical value wins, and failed writes leave the source intact
adversarial_factors: entries expiring before or exactly at nowMs must be deleted without a canonical write, rejecting a blanket dual-write of stale data
a pre-existing unexpired canonical value differs from the legacy value and must win, rejecting replay of older v1 data during an idempotent migration
the cache can throw while publishing the canonical key and the mutation log proves the unexpired legacy source was not deleted first
browser and server identities share resource shapes but require distinct escaped compound keys, while the rollback reader still knows only the resource-only key
the protected cache records mutations and entry deadlines, rejecting cleanup of an unexpired source or extension of its TTL
